title: Lawn care vehicle with zero-turn functionality
abstract: The present invention is related to a riding lawn care vehicle (100) that includes motors (302) to drive wheels (304) in a driving direction and an opposite driving direction, and a steering assembly (310) to control the motors to drive the wheels and steer the riding lawn care vehicle. The riding lawn care vehicle also includes processing circuitry (312) configured to receive user input from the steering assembly (310), and identify a defined turn action including the driving direction for a first of the wheels from the user input. In response, the processing circuitry is configured to command the motors to drive the wheels to execute the defined turn action in which the first of the wheels is driven in the driving direction, a second of the wheels is driven in the opposite driving direction, and both the first and the second of the wheels are driven at a speed that is the same for both.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A riding lawn care vehicle comprising: motors to drive wheels in a driving direction and an opposite driving direction; a steering assembly to control the motors to drive the wheels and steer the riding lawn care vehicle; and processing circuitry configured to at least: receive user input from the steering assembly; identify a defined turn action including the driving direction for a first of the wheels from the user input; and in response, command the motors to drive the wheels to execute the defined turn action in which the first of the wheels is driven in the driving direction, a second of the wheels is driven in the opposite driving direction, and both the first and the second of the wheels are driven at a speed that is the same for both. 2. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 1, wherein the steering assembly includes a steering wheel that is movable to control the motors, and the processing circuitry configured to identify the defined turn action includes the processing circuitry configured to: determine a position of the steering wheel from the user input; and identify the defined turn action based on the position of the steering wheel, in which the steering wheel is in a full-lock position. 3. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 1, wherein the steering assembly includes steering controls that are independently movable to control respective ones of the motors, and the processing circuitry configured to identify the defined turn action includes the processing circuitry configured to: determine positions of the steering controls from the user input; and identify the defined turn action based on the positions of the steering controls, in which the first of the steering controls is in a drive position that indicates the driving direction for the first of the wheels, and the second of the steering controls is in a second position. 4. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 3, wherein the drive position of the first of the steering controls also indicates the speed at which both the first and the second of the wheels are driven to execute the turn event. 5. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 3, wherein the second position of the second of the steering controls is a neutral position that indicates a stationary position of a second of the wheels. 6. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 3, wherein the second position of the second of the steering controls is a second drive position that indicates the opposite driving direction for the second of the wheels. 7. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 6, wherein the drive position of the first of the steering controls also indicates a first speed, and the second position of the second of the steering controls also indicates a second speed, and wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to determine the speed at which both the first and the second of the wheels are driven, based on either or both of the first speed or the second speed. 8. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 3, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to repeatedly determine the positions of the steering controls, and repeatedly command the motors to drive the wheels, and wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to command the motors to cease the defined turn action, in response to movement of the second of the steering controls from the second position. 9. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 8, wherein the second position is a neutral position, and the movement of the second of the steering controls is from the neutral position to a second drive position, and wherein the processing circuitry is configured to command a second of the motors to drive the second of the wheels as indicated by the second drive position, in response to the movement of the second of the steering controls. 10. The riding lawn care vehicle of claim 1, wherein the riding lawn care vehicle further comprises wheel position sensors from which the processing circuitry is configured to receive feedback on position and movement of the wheels, and wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to command the motors to maintain the driving direction of the first of the wheels, the opposite driving direction of the 

second of the wheels, and the speed at which both the first and the second of the wheels are driven, based on the feedback. 11. A method of operating a riding lawn care vehicle that includes motors to drive wheels in a driving direction and an opposite driving direction, and a steering assembly to control the motors to drive the wheels and steer the riding lawn care vehicle, the method comprising: receiving user input from the steering assembly; identifying a defined turn action including the driving direction for a first of the wheels from the user input; and in response, commanding the motors to drive the wheels to execute the defined turn action in which the first of the wheels is driven in the driving direction, a second of the wheels is driven in the opposite driving direction, and both the first and the second of the wheels are driven at a speed that is the same for both. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the steering assembly includes a steering wheel that is movable to control the motors, and identifying the defined turn action includes: determining a position of the steering wheel from the user input; and identifying the defined turn action based on the position of the steering wheel, in which the steering wheel is in a full-lock position. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the steering assembly includes steering controls that are independently movable to control respective ones of the motors, and identifying the defined turn action includes: determining positions of the steering controls from the user input; and identifying the defined turn action based on the positions of the steering controls, in which the first of the steering controls is in a drive position that indicates the driving direction for the first of the wheels, and the second of the steering controls is in a second position. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the drive position of the first of the steering controls also indicates the speed at which both the first and the second of the wheels are driven to execute the turn event. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the second position of the second of the steering controls is a neutral position that indicates a stationary position of a second of the wheels. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the second position of the second of the steering controls is a second drive position that indicates the opposite driving direction for the second of the wheels. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the drive position of the first of the steering controls also indicates a first speed, and the second position of the second of the steering controls also indicates a second speed, and wherein the method further comprises determining the speed at which both the first and the second of the wheels are driven, based on either or both of the first speed or the second speed. 18. The method of claim 13, wherein the positions of the steering controls are repeatedly determined, and the motors are repeatedly commanded to drive the wheels, and wherein the method further comprises commanding the motors to cease the defined turn action, in response to movement of the second of the steering controls from the second position. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the second position is a neutral position, and the movement of the second of the steering controls is from the neutral position to a second drive position, and wherein the method further comprises commanding a second of the motors to drive the second of the wheels as indicated by the second drive position, in response to the movement of the second of the steering controls. 20. The method of claim 11 , wherein the riding lawn care vehicle further includes wheel position sensors, and the method further comprises: receiving feedback on position and movement of the wheels from the wheel position sensors; and 

 commanding the motors to maintain the driving direction of the first of the wheels, the opposite driving direction of the second of the wheels, and the speed at which both the first and the second of the wheels are driven, based on the feedback.
